---
title: "Figure_4"
author: "Charlotte Uden"
date: "2026-01-29"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

ROC and PR curves and reliability diagram

```{r}
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

Load configuration files
- location of model drivers, rds files (containing models), tables of parameter estimates and performance metrics, and figures. 
```{r}
read_properties <- function(file_path) {
  lines <- readLines(file_path)
  lines <- lines[grepl("=", lines)]  # only keep lines with key-value pairs
  key_vals <- strsplit(lines, "=")
  props <- setNames(
    trimws(sapply(key_vals, `[`, 2)),
    trimws(sapply(key_vals, `[`, 1))
  )
  return(props)
}

#config <- read_properties("/path/to/config/file/my_config.properties")  # path to your config file

# Extract values from config
drivers_root <- config[["drivers_root"]]
rds_root <- config[["rds_root"]]
table_root <- config[["table_root"]]
predictions_root <- config[["predictions_root"]]
figure_root <- config[["figure_root"]]

# Use the values to build full paths 
```

```{r}
df <- read.csv(file.path(predictions_root, "fire_predictions_and_observations.csv"))
head(df)
#p = predicitons of fire prob, P_fire
#y = binary observations of fire outcome: 0 or 1
p <- df$p
y <- df$y
```

```{r}
## =========================================================
## Helpers (used internally)
## =========================================================

# Drop NAs and check basic validity
.clean_xy <- function(p, y) {
  if (length(p) != length(y)) {
    stop("p and y must have the same length.")
  }
  ok <- is.finite(p) & is.finite(y)
  p <- p[ok]; y <- y[ok]
  list(p = p, y = y)
}

# Clip probabilities away from 0 and 1 to avoid log(0)
.clip_probs <- function(p, eps = 1e-15) {
  p[p < eps]      <- eps
  p[p > 1 - eps]  <- 1 - eps
  p
}

## =========================================================
## 1) Brier score
## =========================================================
# Mean squared error between probabilities and 0/1 outcomes
brier_score <- function(p, y) {
  dat <- .clean_xy(p, y); p <- dat$p; y <- dat$y
  mean((p - y)^2)
}

## =========================================================
## 2) Brier Skill Score (relative to climatology)
## =========================================================
# Baseline = climatological event probability (mean(y))
# BSS = 1 - BS_model / BS_climatology
brier_skill_score <- function(p, y) {
  dat <- .clean_xy(p, y); p <- dat$p; y <- dat$y
  
  if (length(unique(y)) < 2) {
    warning("y has no variation (all 0s or all 1s); BSS undefined.")
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  
  bs_model <- mean((p - y)^2)
  p_clim   <- mean(y)
  bs_clim  <- mean((p_clim - y)^2)
  
  if (bs_clim == 0) {
    warning("Climatological Brier score is 0; BSS undefined.")
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  
  1 - bs_model / bs_clim
}

## =========================================================
## 3) Logarithmic score (log loss)
## =========================================================
# Negative log-likelihood of Bernoulli outcomes under predicted probabilities
log_loss <- function(p, y, eps = 1e-15) {
  dat <- .clean_xy(p, y); p <- dat$p; y <- dat$y
  p <- .clip_probs(p, eps)
  -mean(y * log(p) + (1 - y) * log(1 - p))
}


## =========================================================
## 4) ROC AUC (rank-based, no packages)
## =========================================================
# Area under ROC curve; threshold-free discrimination metric
roc_auc <- function(p, y) {
  dat <- .clean_xy(p, y); p <- dat$p; y <- dat$y
  
  y_vals <- unique(y)
  if (!all(y_vals %in% c(0, 1))) {
    stop("y must contain only 0 and 1.")
  }
  if (length(unique(y)) < 2) {
    warning("y has no variation (all 0s or all 1s); ROC AUC undefined.")
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  
  # Rank predictions (average ranks for ties)
  r <- rank(p, ties.method = "average")
  
  n_pos <- sum(y == 1)
  n_neg <- sum(y == 0)
  
  # Sum of ranks for positive cases
  sum_r_pos <- sum(r[y == 1])
  
  # Mann-Whitney U statistic -> AUC
  auc <- (sum_r_pos - n_pos * (n_pos + 1) / 2) / (n_pos * n_neg)
  auc
}

## =========================================================
## 5) Precision–Recall AUC (PR-AUC)
## =========================================================
# PR-AUC is more informative under class imbalance (rare fires)
pr_auc <- function(p, y) {
  dat <- .clean_xy(p, y); p <- dat$p; y <- dat$y
  
  y_vals <- unique(y)
  if (!all(y_vals %in% c(0, 1))) {
    stop("y must contain only 0 and 1.")
  }
  P <- sum(y == 1)
  N <- sum(y == 0)
  if (P == 0) {
    warning("No positive events in y; PR-AUC undefined.")
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  
  # Sort by decreasing probability
  o <- order(p, decreasing = TRUE)
  y_sorted <- y[o]
  p_sorted <- p[o]
  
  # Cumulative true positives and false positives
  tp <- cumsum(y_sorted == 1)
  fp <- cumsum(y_sorted == 0)
  
  recall    <- tp / P
  precision <- tp / (tp + fp)
  
  # Add (0, baseline) point for integration
  recall    <- c(0, recall)
  precision <- c(mean(y), precision)  # baseline precision = prevalence
  
  # Trapezoidal rule in (recall, precision) space
  # Note: recall is non-decreasing by construction
  pr_auc_val <- sum(
    0.5 * (precision[-1] + precision[-length(precision)]) *
      diff(recall)
  )
  
  pr_auc_val
}

## =========================================================
## 6) Expected vs observed fire counts
## =========================================================
# Returns a named list: expected (sum of p), observed (sum of y)
expected_observed_counts <- function(p, y) {
  dat <- .clean_xy(p, y); p <- dat$p; y <- dat$y
  list(
    expected = sum(p),
    observed = sum(y)
  )
}

#note: we sum predicted probabilities because simulating 0/1 from the probability (ie sampling from a binomial distribution) adds noise - not good for model verification. 

#expected fire counts are summed rather than stochastically sampled since the expected sum of Bernoulli  trials provides a deterministic and unbiased estimate of aggregate event frequency. 

#later on, for something like forecasting or implementing into a DGVM, we would sample from a binomial distribution - variability contains information. 


```

#ROC curve
```{r}
roc_curve_df <- function(p, y) {
  stopifnot(length(p) == length(y))
  stopifnot(all(y %in% c(0, 1)))

  # Remove NAs
  ok <- is.finite(p) & is.finite(y)
  p <- p[ok]
  y <- y[ok]

  # Sort by decreasing probability
  o <- order(p, decreasing = TRUE)
  p <- p[o]
  y <- y[o]

  P <- sum(y == 1)
  N <- sum(y == 0)

  tp <- cumsum(y == 1)
  fp <- cumsum(y == 0)

  tpr <- tp / P
  fpr <- fp / N

  # Add origin
  data.frame(
    fpr = c(0, fpr),
    tpr = c(0, tpr),
    threshold = c(Inf, p)
  )
}

roc_df <- roc_curve_df(p, y)

#add ROC AUC (area under the curve) to the plot
roc_auc_text <- sprintf("ROC AUC = %.3f", roc_auc(p, y))

p_roc <- ggplot(roc_df, aes(fpr, tpr)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_abline(linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  #coord_equal() +
  labs(
    x = "False Positive Rate",
    y = "True Positive Rate",
    title = "(b)"
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.125, label = roc_auc_text, 
           color = "red", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  theme_bw()

p_roc
```

#PR Curve
```{r}
pr_curve_df <- function(p, y) {
  stopifnot(length(p) == length(y))
  stopifnot(all(y %in% c(0, 1)))

  ok <- is.finite(p) & is.finite(y)
  p <- p[ok]
  y <- y[ok]

  o <- order(p, decreasing = TRUE)
  p <- p[o]
  y <- y[o]

  P <- sum(y == 1)

  tp <- cumsum(y == 1)
  fp <- cumsum(y == 0)

  recall <- tp / P
  precision <- tp / (tp + fp)

  # Add baseline point
  data.frame(
    recall = c(0, recall),
    precision = c(mean(y), precision),
    threshold = c(Inf, p)
  )
}

pr_df <- pr_curve_df(p, y)

#annotate thresholds
annot_thresh <- c(1e-4, 5e-4, 1e-3, 2e-3)
#find closest rows to these thresholds
ann_df <- lapply(annot_thresh, function(t) {
  pr_df %>%
    mutate(diff = abs(threshold - t)) %>%
    slice_min(diff, n = 1)
}) %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  mutate(label = paste0("t=", sprintf("%.6f", threshold)))

pr_auc_text <- sprintf("PR AUC = %.5f", pr_auc(p, y))

p_pr <- ggplot(pr_df, aes(recall, precision)) +
  geom_line(size = 1, color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean(y), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +

  # # annotation points
  # geom_point(data = ann_df,
  #            aes(recall, precision),
  #            color = "red", size = 3) +

  # geom_label(data = ann_df,
  #          aes(recall, precision, label = label),
  #          hjust = -0.1,
  #          vjust = 0,
  #          size = 3,
  #          fill = "white",
  #          label.size = 0) +

  labs(
    x = "Recall",
    y = "Precision",
    title = "(c)"
  ) +

  annotate("text", x = 0.8, y = 0.0006,
           label = pr_auc_text,
           color = "red", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +

  theme_bw()

p_pr



```


#Reliability curve with quantile binning
(without quantile binning, all probabilities are in the first bin)
```{r}
reliability_curve_quantile <- function(p, y, nbins = 10) {
  dat <- .clean_xy(p, y)
  p <- dat$p; y <- dat$y
  
  # quantile-based breaks (to avoid empty bins) - based on the quantiles of predictions
  probs  <- seq(0, 1, length.out = nbins + 1)
  breaks <- quantile(p, probs = probs, na.rm = TRUE) 
  breaks <- unique(breaks)  # in case of ties
  
  df <- data.frame(p = p, y = y) %>%
    mutate(bin = cut(p, breaks = breaks, include.lowest = TRUE)) %>%
    group_by(bin) %>%
    summarise(
      n          = n(),
      mean_pred  = mean(p),
      event_rate = mean(y),
      .groups    = "drop"
    )
  
  # extract numeric bounds
  df$bin_lower <- as.numeric(sub("\\((.+),.*", "\\1", sub("\\[", "(", df$bin)))
  df$bin_upper <- as.numeric(sub(".*,(.+)\\]", "\\1", sub("\\)", "]", df$bin)))
  df$bin_mid   <- (df$bin_lower + df$bin_upper) / 2
  
  df %>%
    dplyr::select(bin_lower, bin_upper, bin_mid, n, mean_pred, event_rate)
}

rel_tab <- reliability_curve_quantile(p, y, nbins = 10)
rel_tab

p_rel <- ggplot(rel_tab, aes(x = mean_pred, y = event_rate)) +
  geom_point(shape = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  xlab("Mean predicted probability") +
  ylab("Observed event frequency") +
  ggtitle("(a)") +
  ylim(0, 0.0007) +
  xlim(0, 0.0007) +
  theme_bw() 

p_rel

# rel_tab <- mutate(rel_tab, n_pred = n*mean_pred, n_event = n*event_rate)
# 
# p_rel <- ggplot(rel_tab, aes(x = n_pred, y = n_event)) +
#   geom_point(shape = 1) +
#   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
#   xlab("Summed predicted probability") +
#   ylab("Summed observed event frequency") +
#   ggtitle("(c)") +
#   ylim(0, 270) +
#   xlim(0, 270) +
#   theme_bw() 
# 
# p_rel
```


```{r}
blank_plot <- ggplot() + theme_void()

grid.arrange(p_roc, p_pr, p_rel, blank_plot, nrow=2, ncol=2, heights=c(1,1), widths=c(1, 1))

layout <- rbind(c(1, 2), 
                c(3, 3))


grid.arrange(p_rel, p_roc, p_pr, layout_matrix = layout)

p <- grid.arrange(p_rel, p_roc, p_pr, layout_matrix = layout)
```

```{r}
ggsave(file.path(figure_root, "Figure_4.png"), plot = p, width = 9, height = 9, dpi = 1100, units = "in")
```
